← All PY handicap examples

PY — TCF derived from integer Portsmouth Yardstick number

TCF = 1000 ÷ PY; otherwise identical to IRC. A lower PY number means a faster boat type.

Scoring system: PY 
Start 14:00:00 (= 50400 s since midnight)

Boat 1 (PY 1000): finish 15:00:00  ET = 3600 s   TCF = 1.000   CT = 3600.00 s  → 1st
Boat 2 (PY  900): finish 14:55:00  ET = 3300 s   TCF = 1.111   CT = 3666.67 s  → 2nd
Boat 3 (PY 1100): finish 15:10:00  ET = 4200 s   TCF = 0.909   CT = 3818.18 s  → 3rd

Boat 2 is the fastest type (PY 900) but finished in the middle;
its higher TCF inflates its corrected time above Boat 1.

Race arithmetic

Gun time: 14:00:00
RankNameSail #PYFinish timeETTCFCTPoints
1 Alpha 1 1000 15:00:00 60m 00s 1.0000 (1000/PY) 60m 00s 1
2 Beta 2 900 14:55:00 55m 00s 1.1111 (1000/PY) 61m 07s 2
3 Gamma 3 1100 15:10:00 70m 00s 0.9091 (1000/PY) 63m 38s 3

Series standings

RankNameSail #R1TotalNet
1 Alpha 1 1 1 1
2 Beta 2 2 2 2
3 Gamma 3 3 3 3
sailscoring.ie
